summary:
The condition causing pure left-sided cardiac dysfunction is more likely due to its specific anatomical impact; among options provided, only aortic stenosis directly affects and obliterates blood flow from the left ventricle exclusively, fitting this description perfectly compared to other listed possibilities which do not isolate their effect solely within one hemi-system. Thus, option b), aortic stenosis, aligns most closely based upon these considerations regarding strict unilateral impairment characteristic required by "pure" designation here. To reiterate succinctly:

- **Key Point:** Only aortic stenosis causes isolated damage primarily affecting just the left side of the heart through direct valve obstruction at critical point leading to pure left-sided failure scenario described accurately under current medical understanding presented herein. ```plaintext
The primary cause of pure left-sided heart failure is aortic stenosis because it specifically narrows the left ventricular outflow tract, whereas other conditions either involve both sides or have less defined effects limited mostly to indirect influences on the left side. Hence, \( \boxed{\text{B}} \).
```
